summary: Large fighter-collision decomp PR centered on src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c. It replaced many NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies, improved several unmatched ftColl functions, added/includes and header prototypes, refined ft/types.h structures and DmgLogEntry typing, and added EntityKind_UNKNOWN. The only loaded feedback is the decomp bot report showing +1688 matched-code bytes, 8 new function matches, 13 unmatched-item improvements, and a .sdata match regression.

key_files.role: Primary decomp target; many ftColl functions changed from NOT_IMPLEMENTED or improved, with collision, damage-log, item-hit, shield-hit, absorb/hurt capsule, and damage processing logic.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c added 1816 deleted 101 hunks 27
raw_diff: ftcoll.c includes added for ft_0881.h, ft_0DF0.h, ftchangeparam.h, it_2725.h, pl_040D.h, player.h, pltrick.h, MSL/trigf.h
raw_diff: NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies replaced for ftColl_80077688, ftColl_80077970, ftColl_80077C60 and other ftColl functions
text_corpus: bot report lists 8 new matches and 13 improvements in main/melee/ft/ftcoll

raw_diff: DmgLogEntry.x20 changed from int to float

what_changed.text: Added typed implementations or substantial bodies for fighter collision functions including item-vs-fighter/shield paths such as ftColl_80077688, ftColl_80077970, and ftColl_80077C60.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftColl_80077688 changed from NOT_IMPLEMENTED to item/hurt/fighter damage handling
raw_diff: ftColl_80077970 signature changed to Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*, HitCapsule* and body added
raw_diff: ftColl_80077C60 signature changed to bool Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*, HitCapsule* and body added

what_changed.text: Recovered or improved type surfaces in ftcoll.h and types.h instead of leaving UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, void*, or padding where the PR body could express the access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftcoll.h prototype updates
raw_diff: ftData_x30, ftDynamics.x8, Fighter.x1670.x24, DmgLogEntry.x20 changes

what_changed.text: Converted several OSReport + HSD_ASSERT or raw __assert sequences into HSD_ASSERTREPORT with preserved messages and line-number constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftColl_80076640 HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0xB7, NULL, "attack power over 500!! %f\n", *dmg)
raw_diff: ftColl_80076764 HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0xF9, NULL, "damage log over %d!!\n", ARRAY_SIZE(dmg_log0))
raw_diff: inlineB2 and other ftcoll.c sites use HSD_ASSERTREPORT

what_changed.text: Used matching-oriented source-shape tactics including PAD_STACK, goto retfalse, scoped #pragma dont_inline, and local helper/temporary struct shapes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(32) in ftColl_80076ED8; PAD_STACK(16) in ftColl_80077688 and ftColl_80077970; larger PAD_STACK in ftColl_80079EA8
raw_diff: goto retfalse label introduced in ftColl_80076ED8
raw_diff: #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop blocks around several functions

what_changed.text: Changed damage-log bookkeeping: dmg_log indices became int, a ftColl_80076ED8 entry target was corrected from dmg_log1[dmg_log1_idx] to dmg_log0[dmg_log0_idx], and DmgLogEntry.x20 became float.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static size_t dmg_log0_idx/dmg_log1_idx changed to static int
raw_diff: DmgLogEntry* damageLog2 changed to &dmg_log0[dmg_log0_idx]
raw_diff: DmgLogEntry.x20 changed from int to float in types.h

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were updated alongside recovered function bodies, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS surfaces.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftcoll.h prototype changes for ftColl_80077970, ftColl_80077C60, ftColl_80078538, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_80079C70

smart_moves.text: The PR preferred typed fields and structs for several recovered accesses instead of keeping raw unknown pointers or padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftData.x30 struct, ftDynamics.x8 typed pointer, Fighter.x1670.x24, DmgLogEntry.x20

smart_moves.text: Report/assert cleanup used HSD_ASSERTREPORT forms that preserve report text and arguments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ASSERTREPORT conversions with attack power, damage log, tip log, too many hurt capsules, and too many x1670 entries messages

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a function body proves call signatures, update the owning header in the same PR rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftColl_80077970 and ftColl_80077C60 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures
raw_diff: ftColl_80078998 and ftColl_80079C70 prototypes refined

decomp_lessons.lesson: For ftcoll damage-log work, field typing matters for matching and reviewability; this PR changed DmgLogEntry.x20 from int to float and index variables from size_t to int while updating access sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: DmgLogEntry.x20 int to float
raw_diff: dmg_log0_idx and dmg_log1_idx size_t to int

decomp_lessons.lesson: Raw OSReport + assert sequences can often map to HSD_ASSERTREPORT when the report string and assert line are preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: OSReport plus HSD_ASSERT converted to HSD_ASSERTREPORT at attack power and damage-log sites
decomp_standards: assert-report-macros

naming_conventions.text: The PR mostly retained address-style global/function names such as ftColl_80077688 and unknown field names such as x19A0_shieldDamageTaken/x221D_b6 where semantics were incomplete, while using short local role names like fp, item, hit, hurt, gobj, entry, and dmg.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftColl_* address-style function names retained
raw_diff: local variables in added bodies include fp, item, hit, hurt, gobj, entry, dmg

naming_conventions.text: New enum value EntityKind_UNKNOWN uses the existing EntityKind_* prefix but all-caps UNKNOWN; the slice does not explain whether this is a durable naming convention.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: EntityKind_UNKNOWN added to enum EntityKind

assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence shows use but not why each PAD_STACK value was necessary; keep as observed tactic, not a global style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline blocks were moved/added around several ftcoll functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop around ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_8007BC90, ftColl_8007BE3C
raw_diff: previous pragma around ftColl_80078538 removed
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: No human review or adjacent-function regression discussion is loaded; this remains a matching exception requiring narrow verification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Control-flow was sometimes reshaped to match with explicit gotos and labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftColl_80076ED8 introduces goto retfalse and retfalse label
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Do not generalize goto use beyond this observed matching case.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Small repeated computations were manually inlined in added source, such as getEnvDmg-style float-to-int damage conversion.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: comment 'inline getEnvDmg' in ftColl_8007BE3C
raw_diff: repeated pattern: if damage nonzero and integer part zero, use 1
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence does not prove whether these should remain manual inlines outside this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update owning headers when recovered ftcoll bodies prove signatures

evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftcoll.h prototype changes
decomp_standards: truthful-headers-and-includes

evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_ASSERTREPORT conversions at attack power, damage log, tip log sites
